sections.paragraph:
συνέρχομαι [σύν, ἔρχομαι]

—1. ‘come together as a collection of pers.’

—a. of joining at a scene gather Mk 3:20; 6:33 v.l.; Lk 5:15; J 18:20; Ac 2:6; 5:16; 16:13.

—b. of gathering for business meet, assemble Ac 1:6; 22:30; 1 Cor 11:17f, 20; 14:23, 26. The idea of coming to a place combines with business intention Ac 25:17.

—2. ‘come together in a close personal relationship’, come together

—a. of sexual intimacy 1 Cor 7:5 v.l.

—b. of entry into a marriage relationship πρὶν ἢ συνελϑεῖν αὐτούς before they were married Mt 1:18.

—3. ‘come/go together with’, accompany Lk 23:55; J 11:33; Ac 1:21; 9:39; 10:23, 45; 11:12; 15:38; 21:16.
